title: House of Thul

The '''House of Thul''', also known as '''House Thul''', was an ancient noble house of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], founded over 3,600 years before the birth of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], family members [[Bornan Thul|Bornan]] and [[Aryn Dro Thul|Aryn Thul]] founded [[Bornaryn Trading]], which would exist as a major trading entity for decades to come.

When Alderaan was [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed by the Galactic Empire]], only a handful of House Thul family members were off-world.

==History==
===Cold War===
[[File:Elana_Thul.png|thumb|left|140px|[[Elana Thul]], Lady of House Thul]]
House Thul was founded long before the [[Great Galactic War]] by a wealthy and ambitious merchant who bought his way into the nobility. Around [[3700 BBY/Legends|3700 BBY]], when House Thul was unable to marry into royalty, the house made a desperate bid for power by swapping an Organa infant for a Thul one and supported the changeling as a potential bride for the crown prince. However, a young Jedi exposed the plot, creating a scandal that forced the [[Alderaanian Parliament]] to exile the entire house from Alderaan.<ref>{{TORcite|type=codex|House Thul}}</ref>

Despite attempts to maintain its pride and culture in exile, House Thul became impoverished and isolated in their offworld estates. Decades later, they were contacted by the [[Imperial Diplomatic Service]], promising the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire's]] support in restoring them to glory.<ref>{{TORcite|type=codex|House Thul}}</ref>

When Alderaan had withdrawn from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], House Thul found itself in a bloody civil war against the Republic-backed [[House of Organa/Legends|House Organa]]. House Thul became even further entangled in Sith affairs when [[Darth Baras]]' [[Empire's Wrath|apprentice]] arrived on Alderaan to hunt down the family of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Jaesa Willsaam]]. The House benefited greatly in its quest for the Alderaanian throne due to the various interventions of the [[Empire's Wrath|Sith Warrior]]. The [[Darth Nox|descendant]] of [[Aloysius Kallig]], who was the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] of [[Darth Zash]] also met with House Thul in Zash's search for an artifact that once belonged to [[Tulak Hord]].

===Galactic Civil War===
Over three millennia later, during the Galactic Civil War, Bornan and Aryn Thul founded Bornaryn Trading together. The corporation involved shipping, [[Tibanna/Legends|Tibanna]] gas processing, and [[Droid/Legends|droid]] production; and would come to include subsidiaries [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]] and [[Xtib]].

When Alderaan was destroyed by the Galactic Empire, Bornan and Aryn Thul were among the few Alderaanians who were off-world. Their company survived, and would continue to operate until and after the [[Swarm War]]; Bornan and Aryn's child, [[Raynar Thul|Raynar]], trained at [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Jedi Praxeum|Jedi Academy]], before becoming a key figure during the Swarm War.
